reviews.review_msg: Sucks. He was not there at all to help us theorugh anything. the TA only gave announcements and barely helped. really wish I didnt take this class. Dont take this is you dont have to

The textbook written by him is ridiculously long for the information he is trying to convey and mostly based on opinion. Some studies are cited but with many, he concludes that there is insufficient information to know the truth. For a 100 level course, it is hard to follow along and Im in my fourth year. I would recommend him to use less opinion.
The textbook written by Tony is material based on his opinion and he just goes on rants about the material. He's not accessible for help at all. The TA's do not give good feedback on the marking.
The textbook written by Tony is material based on his option and he just goes on rants about the material. He's not accessible for help at all. the TA's do not give good feedback on the marking.

Way too much detail and way too much to know for an intro level course. Textbook is not helpful in identifying terms, so you actually have to read every sentence practically. Midterm covers 12 chapters with extensive detail. Assignments hardly count, it's all the exams. Tony was absent from providing any help, and TA was bad too. good luck

She was an excellent professor, she was very engaging and enjoyable to listen to at 8:30AM, all grading criteria was clear and what you put into the class is what you get out grade wise. I would recommend her for 343 or any other class she teaches. She is good at what she does.

Do NOT take BPK342 or 343 with this professor. She does not care about actually teaching, but rather wants to show off her knowledge on Exercise Performance. She does not create lecture videos and posts content directly onto the canvas page (with lots of spelling mistakes). Rarely engages with the class through discussion posts, announcements, etc.

BPK343 - Fun labs, don't like assignments (case study -no practice with doing it + she got mad we did bad on it). Grading was kinda strict in my opinion for tests and assignments. Great lectures though, the info I learned was great, I just feel like I didn't get to prove that with the testing she had. Unclear on what is tested + lots of reading

The course is very useful in daily life, but it is also quite challenging. Lectures and material get really compact and abstract, especially after the midterm. The assignments were fairly easy, while the exams, especially the final, were really hard. Personally, I would only take this course if you took a bio course or is passionate in working out.
